turns.user_message: I made a fresh pot of coffee this morning and just stared at the steam rising from the mug without taking a single sip. It used to be my favorite part of the day, but today it just felt like a chore I had to finish. I sat there for twenty minutes trying to force myself to care about the smell, but my brain just stayed completely blank. I guess it is just one of those off days where nothing really clicks into place.

That same cold mug has been sitting on my counter for days now, mirroring how I just keep going through the motions without tasting anything. I keep waiting for the spark to return when I wake up, but every morning just slides right past me without leaving a trace. I tried brewing different blends and sitting by the window like I always do, but it all feels completely hollow now. I am just moving through the hours like I am stuck on repeat, wondering when the warmth will actually fade back in.

It is exhausting to sit in this quiet emptiness and realize that absolutely nothing reaches me anymore, not even the steam from my morning cup. I keep trying to trick myself into feeling something by changing my routine, but the heavy fog just settles right back over everything. Every single moment stretches out into this long, flat line where I am physically present but completely disconnected from the world around me. I just feel like a ghost drifting through my own days, waiting for a reaction that never actually arrives.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This just started recently.
